annotations.label: Riemann hypothesis

anchor.snippet: The real part of every nontrivial zero of the Riemann zeta function is

annotations.note: `RiemannHypothesis` is defined as the proposition that every non-trivial zero of `riemannZeta` has real part 1/2.

annotations.label: Trivial zeros of zeta

anchor.snippet: These are called its trivial zeros

annotations.note: Mathlib proves ζ(−2(n+1)) = 0 but does not introduce a named definition for the set of trivial zeros.

annotations.label: Nontrivial zeros of zeta

anchor.snippet: which are called nontrivial zeros

annotations.note: The exclusion condition for non-trivial zeros appears inline in `RiemannHypothesis` but is not separately named; `riemannZetaZeros` defines all zeros, not just non-trivial ones.

annotations.label: Critical line

anchor.snippet: all the nontrivial zeros lie on the critical line

annotations.note: The condition `s.re = 1/2` is used inside `RiemannHypothesis`, but the critical line is not given its own definition.

annotations.label: Riemann zeta function (Dirichlet series)

anchor.snippet: by the absolutely convergent infinite series

annotations.note: Theorem establishing `riemannZeta s = ∑' n, 1 / n^s` for `1 < re s`.

annotations.label: Euler product formula

anchor.snippet: it equals the Euler product

annotations.note: States ∏' p prime, (1 − p^(−s))⁻¹ = riemannZeta s for re s > 1.

annotations.label: Zeta–eta relation

anchor.snippet: the series for the zeta function and the Dirichlet eta function satisfy the relation

annotations.note: No Dirichlet eta function appears in Mathlib and no zeta–eta identity was located.

annotations.label: Functional equation of zeta

anchor.snippet: this extension of the zeta function satisfies the functional equation

annotations.note: Gives ζ(1−s) = 2(2π)^(−s) Γ(s) cos(πs/2) ζ(s); `completedRiemannZeta_one_sub` gives the symmetric form for Λ.

annotations.label: Trivial zeros at negative even integers

anchor.snippet: these are the zeta function's trivial zeros

annotations.note: States ζ(−2(n+1)) = 0 for every natural n.

annotations.label: Value zeta(0) = -1/2

anchor.snippet: The value ζ (0) = −1/2 is not determined by the functional equation

annotations.note: Proves `riemannZeta 0 = -1/2`.
